articles.title: Developer’s Guide to AI Agent Protocols
articles.snippet: This blog post introduces a suite of six protocols, such as MCP and A2A, designed to eliminate custom integration code by standardizing how AI agents access data and communicate. Using a "kitchen manager" agent as a practical example, it demonstrates how these tools handle complex tasks like real-time inventory checks, wholesale commerce via UCP, and secure payment authorization through AP2. By leveraging the Agent Development Kit (ADK), developers can also implement A2UI and AG-UI to deliver in
articles.ai_summary: A new suite of six AI agent protocols has been introduced to standardize data access and communication, simplifying complex tasks for developers. This is significant as it reduces the need for custom integration code, making AI development more efficient.
articles.why_it_matters: Streamlines AI development by standardizing protocols, enhancing efficiency.

articles.title: Build a smart financial assistant with LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1
articles.snippet: This blog post introduces a workflow for extracting high-quality data from complex, unstructured documents by combining LlamaParse with Gemini 3.1 models. It demonstrates an event-driven architecture that uses Gemini 3.1 Pro for agentic parsing of dense financial tables and Gemini 3.1 Flash for cost-effective summarization. By following the provided tutorial, developers can build a personal finance assistant capable of transforming messy brokerage statements into structured, human-readable insig
articles.ai_summary: A tutorial has been released for building a smart financial assistant using LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1, which can transform unstructured financial data into structured formats. This development is crucial for improving personal finance management through automation.
articles.why_it_matters: Empowers developers to create advanced financial tools that enhance data usability.

articles.title: Jump to play: Building with Gemini & MediaPipe
articles.snippet: The provided workflow streamlines motion-controlled game development by using Gemini Canvas to rapidly prototype mechanics like the MediaPipe Pose Landmarker through high-level prompting. Developers can refine these prototypes in Google AI Studio by optimizing for low-latency "lite" models and stable tracking points, such as shoulder landmarks, to ensure responsive gameplay. The process concludes by using Gemini Code Assist to refactor experimental code into a modular, production-ready applicati
articles.ai_summary: A workflow for motion-controlled game development using Gemini and MediaPipe has been introduced, allowing developers to prototype game mechanics quickly. This innovation is significant as it enhances the responsiveness and quality of gaming experiences.
articles.why_it_matters: Accelerates game development, enabling more interactive and engaging gameplay.

articles.title: Closing the knowledge gap with agent skills
articles.snippet: To bridge the gap between static model knowledge and rapidly evolving software practices, Google DeepMind developed a "Gemini API developer skill" that provides agents with live documentation and SDK guidance. Evaluation results show a massive performance boost, with the gemini-3.1-pro-preview model jumping from a 28.2% to a 96.6% success rate when equipped with the skill. This lightweight approach demonstrates how giving models strong reasoning capabilities and access to a "source of truth" can
articles.ai_summary: Google DeepMind has developed a Gemini API developer skill that significantly improves AI agents' performance by providing live documentation and SDK guidance. This advancement highlights the importance of real-time knowledge for enhancing AI capabilities.
articles.why_it_matters: Boosts AI performance by bridging knowledge gaps with live documentation.

articles.title: Announcing ADK for Java 1.0.0: Building the Future of AI Agents in Java
articles.snippet: Google has released version 1.0.0 of the Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Java, introducing powerful new features like Google Maps grounding, built-in URL fetching, and a standardized Agent2Agent protocol for cross-framework collaboration. The update enhances agent control through a new "App" and "Plugin" architecture, which allows for global logging, automated context window management via event compaction, and "Human-in-the-Loop" workflows for action confirmations. Additionally, the release pro
articles.ai_summary: Google has launched version 1.0.0 of the Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Java, featuring new tools for agent control and cross-framework collaboration. This release is significant as it enhances the development of AI agents in Java, promoting innovation.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances AI agent development in Java, fostering innovation in the field.

articles.title: Ongoing supply-chain attack 'explicitly targeting' security, dev tools
articles.snippet: Vendor confirms repo data exposure after Lapsus$ claims source code, secrets dump Software security testing outfit Checkmarx has become the latest organization caught up in an ongoing attack on security-tool providers. The biz said data posted online appears to have come from one of its GitHub repositories after the Lapsus$ extortion crew claimed to have dumped the company’s source code, secrets, and other sensitive data.…
articles.ai_summary: Checkmarx has confirmed a data breach involving its GitHub repositories, following claims by the Lapsus$ group that they have leaked sensitive source code and secrets. This incident underscores ongoing vulnerabilities in security tools.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights critical security risks in software development tools and practices.

articles.title: Show HN: I replaced a memory app with two Markdown files and a Git repo
articles.snippet: I got tired of re-explaining myself to AI tools every session. Claude forgets me. Cursor forgets me. Switch from one to another and you're back to zero.Existing solutions (Nowledge Mem, Mem0) want you to install apps, configure MCP servers, run local LLMs, an…
articles.ai_summary: A user shares their experience of replacing a memory app with two Markdown files and a Git repository, aiming for better continuity and less reliance on AI tools.
articles.why_it_matters: Demonstrates innovative approaches to personal knowledge management.

articles.title: Maine Governor Blocks First State Freeze on New Data Centers
articles.snippet: The Democratic governor of Maine, Janet Mills, on Friday vetoed a bill that would have made it the first U.S. state to impose a moratorium on large new data centers, even as local opposition to the electricity-hungry facilities grows. The …
articles.ai_summary: Maine Governor Janet Mills vetoed a bill to impose a moratorium on new data centers, despite growing local opposition to their environmental impact and energy consumption.
articles.why_it_matters: Reflects ongoing tensions between tech development and environmental concerns.

articles.title: toposync-vision-cuda added to PyPI
articles.snippet: Toposync application bundle with ONNX Runtime CUDA acceleration.
articles.ai_summary: The toposync-vision-cuda package has been added to PyPI, offering ONNX Runtime CUDA acceleration for enhanced performance in machine learning applications.
articles.why_it_matters: Improves efficiency in deploying machine learning models with CUDA support.

articles.title: Show HN: WaveletLM – wavelet-based, attention-free model with O(n log n) scaling
articles.snippet: WaveletLM is a wavelet-based, attention-free architecture that replaces self-attention with learned lifting wavelet decomposition, a Fast Walsh-Hadamard Transform, per-scale gated spectral mixing with SwiGLU activation, an inverse FWHT, and wavelet reconstruc…
articles.ai_summary: WaveletLM introduces a new architecture that eliminates self-attention in favor of wavelet-based techniques, promising improved efficiency with O(n log n) scaling. This innovation could reshape how models handle data processing.
articles.why_it_matters: Revolutionizes model efficiency, potentially impacting future AI architectures.

articles.title: vox-mcp 0.2.0
articles.snippet: Multi-model AI gateway for MCP clients — routes prompts to Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, DeepSeek, Moonshot, OpenRouter, and local models with conversation memory
articles.ai_summary: The release of vox-mcp 0.2.0 enhances AI capabilities by providing a multi-model gateway that routes prompts across various AI platforms, improving user interaction with conversation memory features. This update signifies a step towards more integrated AI systems.
articles.why_it_matters: Facilitates seamless interaction across multiple AI models, enhancing user experience.

articles.title: AI funding pressures ease as cash flow growth outpaces spending: JPMorgan
articles.ai_summary: JPMorgan reports that AI funding pressures are easing as the growth in cash flow from AI companies begins to outpace their spending. This trend indicates a more sustainable financial environment for AI startups.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals a healthier financial landscape for AI startups, encouraging further innovation.
